article_title: California Subpoenas Developer After Trump Administration Pays $120 Million to Kill Offshore Wind Project
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: California Subpoenas Developer After Trump Administration Pays $120 Million to Kill Offshore Wind Project
content_preview.summary: The Interior Department paid Golden State Wind $120 million to abandon its Morro Bay lease; California subpoenaed the developer and its attorney general anticipates litigation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The California Energy Commission subpoenaed Golden State Wind on May 4, 2026, demanding records about the company's agreement with the U.S. Department of the Interior to abandon a floating offshore wind project off the state's Central Coast — a deal in which the federal government agreed to pay out approximately $120 million in taxpayer funds to end the lease. The action is part of a broader Trump administration campaign that has now committed roughly $1.8 billion to retire four off...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with appropriate depth (1083 words, within the 400–1200 range). Clear section hierarchy covering the subpoena, the deal terms, California's legal response, the broader buyout campaign, legal questions, and the state's ongoing ambitions. The 'What We Don't Know' section demonstrates good journalistic transparency. Tone is neutral throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources fetched successfully. source-0 (energy.ca.gov): CEC official press release verified — confirms subpoena date of May 4, $120 million payout, lease OCS-P 0564, Morro Bay location, CEC Chair David Hochschild quotes, and the oil/gas reinvestment condition. source-1 (kpbs.org): KPBS article verified — confirms CEC subpoena, Hochschild quotes, AG Rob Bonta's anticipated litigation, TotalEnergies $1B deal, Congressional investigation by Huffman and Raskin. source-2 (utilitydive.com, /819329/): Utility Dive verified — confirms Golden State Wind co-ownership (Canada Pension Funds Investment Board and Ocean Winds/Engie/EDP), project's 2 GW capacity, $24M workforce training bid credit, $6M community benefits, CEC letter details, and anticipated litigation. source-3 (edf.org): EDF statement verified — confirms 1.1 million homes estimate, 8,000 high-road jobs projection, $24 million workforce training, Michael Colvin quote verified verbatim. source-4 (utilitydive.com, /818994/): Utility Dive 'dangerous precedent' article verified — confirms $1.8B total, 8.6 GW capacity, Liz Klein and Tony Irish quotes, Interior Dept spokesperson quote, Bluepoint Wind/$765M LNG detail. MISATTRIBUTION FOUND: Article states Klein 'called it reckless, expensive, and irresponsible' but the Utility Dive source clearly attributes the phrase 'reckless, expensive, and irresponsible' to an Interior Department spokesperson, not to Klein. Klein's actual words were 'illegal' and 'no legal basis.' source-5 (nwf.org): NWF press release verified — confirms Amber Hewett quote verbatim, $120M/$765M deal terms, CPP Investments ownership. source-6 (cleantechnica.com): CleanTechnica article verified — confirms AB 525, 5 GW by 2030 / 25 GW by 2045 goals, 200 GW NREL estimate, $228M Proposition 4 port funding, $42.75M CEC port grant for five ports (Humboldt, Long Beach, Oakland, Richmond, San Luis Obispo).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts are accurate and well-sourced. One misattribution identified in the Legal Questions section: the phrase 'reckless, expensive, and irresponsible' is attributed to Liz Klein but was actually stated by an Interior Department spokesperson in a Utility Dive email response. Klein said the deal is 'illegal' with 'no legal basis' — which the article correctly quotes — but should not have had the Interior Dept's own language added to her characterizations. This is a subordinate body claim, not in the headline or summary. All other claims — deal terms, subpoena dates, ownership structure, financial figures, capacity targets — verified accurately against sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The article presents both critical perspectives (Klein, Irish, EDF, NWF, CEC, AG Bonta) and the Interior Department's defense. No editorializing or loaded language in the article's own voice.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage found. Existing offshore wind articles cover different events (court rulings against stop-work orders in 2026-03, Vineyard Wind litigation, international offshore projects). This is the first coverage of the California Energy Commission's subpoena and the Golden State Wind deal specifically.
concerns: One misattribution in the Legal Questions section: the phrase 'reckless, expensive, and irresponsible' is attributed to Liz Klein but was said by an Interior Dept spokesperson in Utility Dive's source-4. A corrections record is being filed.
recommendations: Add energy.ca.gov, kpbs.org, edf.org, and nwf.org to the source allowlist — all are credible primary sources (government agency, public radio, environmental nonprofits) appropriate for energy policy coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong, well-researched News article on an important energy policy story. A single misattribution of a quote to the wrong speaker (a government spokesperson's words ascribed to a critic) warrants APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS rather than REJECT, as the overall substance is sound, the error is limited to one subordinate sentence, and it can be honestly corrected in a corrections record.
